title: Pfizer Financial Summary From 2025 Annual Report
text: For 2025, Pfizer reported revenue of $62.579 billion, compared with $63.627 billion in 2024 and $59.553 billion in 2023. Reported net income was $7.771 billion, compared with $8.031 billion in 2024. Reported diluted EPS was $1.36, down from $1.41 in 2024. Adjusted income was $18.406 billion, up 4%, and adjusted diluted EPS was $3.22, also up 4%. Net cash provided by operating activities was $11.704 billion, and Pfizer paid $9.771 billion of cash dividends. The company's largest 2025 products included Eliquis, Prevnar, Vyndaqel, Comirnaty, Ibrance, Paxlovid, Xtandi, Padcev, Nurtec ODT/Vydura, and Xeljanz. Pfizer invested $10.4 billion in internal R&D and approximately $8.8 billion in business development transactions, mainly reflecting the Metsera acquisition and the 3SBio in-licensing deal. Cost-reduction programs helped improve efficiency, with the cost realignment program expected to deliver about $5.7 billion in net cost savings by the end of 2026. Key risks include drug pricing pressure, loss of exclusivity, generic and biosimilar competition, clinical trial failure, regulatory delays, COVID product demand declines, debt and deleveraging, and integration risk from acquisitions.
